---
title: "02.preprocessing_missing_imputation"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

This part audits missing data patterns and prepares imputation outputs. 

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)

# Load cleaned objects produced by 01_setup.Rmd
baseline <- readRDS("data/processed/baseline_clean.rds")
long <- readRDS("data/processed/long_clean.rds")

# Output folders
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create("outputs/missing", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Quick sanity checks
cat("Loaded cleaned datasets.\n")
cat("baseline dim:", dim(baseline), "\n")
cat("long dim:", dim(long), "\n")
cat("Unique subjects baseline:", n_distinct(baseline$SUBJECT_ID), "\n")
cat("Unique subjects long:", n_distinct(long$SUBJECT_ID), "\n")
```

Missingness audit: we compute the missing rate for each variable in both the baseline and the longitudinal datasets and exported the results as CSV tables for documentation and reporting purposes.
```{r missingness-audit}
# Baseline missing rate per variable
miss_base <- baseline %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ mean(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "missing_rate") %>%
  arrange(desc(missing_rate))

cat("\nTop 10 baseline missing rates:\n")
print(head(miss_base, 10))

readr::write_csv(miss_base, "outputs/missing/missing_baseline.csv")

# Long missing rate per variable
miss_long <- long %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ mean(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "missing_rate") %>%
  arrange(desc(missing_rate))

cat("\nTop 10 longitudinal missing rates:\n")
print(head(miss_long, 10))

readr::write_csv(miss_long, "outputs/missing/missing_longitudinal.csv")
```
Missingness by timepoint (CYCLE x MONTH) for key longitudinal outcomes (MMDs, HIT-6, HADS-A/D, INT and MIDAS when available), in order to assess whetehr missing data patterns varied across follow-up visits.
```{r}
# Define key outcomes (use only those that exist)
key_vars <- c("MMDs", "HIT6", "HADSA", "HADSD", "INT", "MIDAS")
key_vars <- intersect(key_vars, names(long))

# Compute missingness by cycle and month
miss_by_time <- long %>%
  group_by(CYCLE, MONTH) %>%
  summarise(
    across(all_of(key_vars), ~ mean(is.na(.)), .names = "miss_{.col}"),
            .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
  arrange(CYCLE, MONTH)

cat("\nMissingness by cycle/month (first rows):\n")
print(head(miss_by_time, 10))

# Save for reporting
readr::write_csv(miss_by_time, "outputs/missing/missing_by_cycle_month.csv")

```
MICE diagnostics: md.pattern on a baseline subset
```{r mdpattern-run, fig.width=10, fig.height=6, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
if (!requireNamespace("mice", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("mice")
library(mice)

# Select variables (only those available)
pattern_vars <- intersect(
  c("AGE_OF_ONSET", "BMI", "HEIGHT", "FAMILIARITY", "Aura", "Psycopathological"),
  names(baseline)
)

cat("\nVariables used for md.pattern:\n")
print(pattern_vars)

if (length(pattern_vars) >= 2) {

  tmp_plot <- baseline %>% select(all_of(pattern_vars))

  # Create short labels for plotting (only rename columns that exist)
  short_names <- c(
    "AGE_OF_ONSET" = "onset",
    "BMI" = "bmi",
    "HEIGHT" = "height",
    "FAMILIARITY" = "fam",
    "Aura" = "aura",
    "Psycopathological" = "psycho"
  )

  # Apply renaming safely
  tmp_plot <- tmp_plot %>%
    rename_with(~ unname(short_names[.x]),
                .cols = intersect(names(tmp_plot), names(short_names)))

  # Make text smaller and increase top margin
  op <- par(cex = 0.9, mar = c(5, 4, 6, 2) + 0.1)

  md.pattern(tmp_plot)

  par(op)

} else {
  cat("\nNot enough variables found for md.pattern.\n")
  cat("Available baseline columns are:\n")
  print(names(baseline))
}

```


Export md.pattern table: this step saves the md.pattern output as a table. It is useful for reporting missingness patterns withput relying on the plot. 
```{r}
if (length(pattern_vars) >= 2) {

  # Recreate the same temporary dataset used for plotting
  tmp <- baseline %>% select(all_of(pattern_vars))

  # Short variable names (same as Step 4)
  short_names <- c(
    "AGE_OF_ONSET" = "onset",
    "BMI" = "bmi",
    "HEIGHT" = "height",
    "FAMILIARITY" = "fam",
    "Aura" = "aura",
    "Psycopathological" = "psycho"
  )

  # Apply renaming safely (only existing columns)
  tmp <- tmp %>%
    rename_with(
      ~ unname(short_names[.x]),
      .cols = intersect(names(tmp), names(short_names))
    )

  # Compute md.pattern without plotting
  md_tab <- md.pattern(tmp, plot = FALSE)

  # Save table to CSV for the report
  write.csv(md_tab,
            "outputs/missing/mdpattern_baseline_subset.csv",
            row.names = TRUE)

  cat("\nmd.pattern table saved in outputs/missing/mdpattern_baseline_subset.csv\n")

} else {
  cat("\nStep 5 skipped: not enough variables for md.pattern export.\n")
}
```
Baseline imputation (MICE)
After inspecting missingenss patterns in a subset of baseline variables (md. pattern), we run a multivariate imputation by chained equations (MICE) model on the selected baseline covariates.

The goal is to obtain one completed baseline dataset ('baseline_imp') that we will later merge with the longitudinal data for the main analyses.


```{r mice-baseline-main, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
library(mice)

# Select baseline vars to impute (keep it aligned with your md.pattern subset)
mice_vars <- intersect(
  c("SUBJECT_ID", "AGE_OF_ONSET", "BMI", "FAMILIARITY", "Aura", "Psycopathological"),
  names(baseline)
)

cat("\nVariables used for baseline MICE:\n")
print(mice_vars)

base_mice <- baseline %>% select(all_of(mice_vars))

# Convert categorical vars to factors
cat_vars <- intersect(c("FAMILIARITY", "Aura", "Psycopathological"), names(base_mice))
base_mice <- base_mice %>% mutate(across(all_of(cat_vars), as.factor))

# Do not impute SUBJECT_ID
meth <- make.method(base_mice)
meth["SUBJECT_ID"] <- ""

pred <- make.predictorMatrix(base_mice)
pred[, "SUBJECT_ID"] <- 0
pred["SUBJECT_ID", ] <- 0

set.seed(123)
imp <- mice(base_mice, m = 5, maxit = 20, method = meth, predictorMatrix = pred)

# Diagnostics: trace plots
plot(imp)

# Diagnostics: density plots for numeric vars (if present)
if ("BMI" %in% names(base_mice)) densityplot(imp, ~ BMI)
if ("AGE_OF_ONSET" %in% names(base_mice)) densityplot(imp, ~ AGE_OF_ONSET)

# Extract one completed dataset
baseline_imp <- complete(imp, 1)

# Save outputs
saveRDS(imp, "data/processed/baseline_mice_object.rds")
saveRDS(baseline_imp, "data/processed/baseline_imputed.rds")

cat("\nSaved baseline_imp to data/processed/baseline_imputed.rds\n")

```
